context.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?
context.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?

decision.primary_crux: The move from 0.825 to 0.835 is best treated as repricing rather than a broken thesis: BTC is still more likely than not to close above $72,000 on the April 16 noon ET Binance minute, but with only about a 2.4% cushion and a single-minute settlement rule, the updated market price now sits even further above bounded fair value.
secondary_cruxes: There is no major contract blocker; the disagreement remains calibration of one-day downside risk into one exact Binance minute.
The refresh added price movement, not new governing evidence that would justify raising the probability estimate.
Residual UI-versus-API settlement-surface ambiguity remains minor; the economically decisive issue is still ordinary BTC volatility relative to the modest cushion.

valuation.compression_reason: No new bounded evidence justifies changing the prior probability range; the refresh is primarily a market repricing against the same exact-minute settlement risk.

portfolio_constraints: Do not add fresh exposure at the updated market price.
Treat exact-minute Binance settlement risk as the dominant execution constraint even when spot remains above strike.
Reopen only if price cheapens materially or a fresher same-venue check near settlement materially improves the cushion.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: Would require a much cheaper price and still a fresh Binance check near settlement.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: Potential value zone only if same-venue price state remains comfortably above strike.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Bounded fair-value zone; maintain watch-only hold posture.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Above fair value for a one-minute settlement contract with meaningful path risk.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Avoid or exit at rich prices because exact-minute downside tail dominates residual certainty.

thesis_breakers: BTC falls back toward or below 72k on Binance before the Apr 16 fixing window.
A concrete downside macro or crypto catalyst emerges that materially raises the probability of a sub-72k settlement minute.
Evidence appears that Binance's operative settlement surface differs materially from the assumed UI/API mapping.
time_breakers: A fresh direct Binance observation closer to Apr 16 12:00 ET should supersede this packet before any action.
The actual Apr 16 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close fully resolves and obsoletes this judgment.
market_structure_breakers: Market price moves materially again without corresponding same-venue spot support, changing the value comparison.
Binance operational or microstructure issues emerge near settlement.
reversal_conditions: Upgrade only if price becomes materially cheaper relative to fair value or if a fresh pre-fix Binance check shows a meaningfully larger cushion.
Downgrade if BTC loses cushion or if downside volatility or catalyst risk increases.
Do not auto-reverse from this packet.

key_uncertainties: The true probability of a roughly 2-3% downside move landing exactly at the settlement minute.
Whether the market embeds fresher venue-specific information than the bounded package contains.
How much residual UI-versus-API settlement-surface uncertainty matters in practice.
what_would_change_my_mind: A fresh Binance check near settlement showing sustained trade materially above 72k would move me closer to market.
A move back toward 72k or evidence of elevated near-term downside volatility would lower fair value materially.
A materially cheaper market price with unchanged Binance cushion would create a cleaner edge.
reasons_to_pass: The updated market price is now further above bounded fair value.
This remains a one-minute single-venue settlement contract where path risk matters more than broad BTC direction.
The refresh added price movement, not new evidence supporting a higher probability.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: BTC is still more likely than not to finish above $72,000 on the April 16 Binance noon minute, but the market's move up to 0.835 only worsens the prior below-market valuation view, so the disciplined output remains watch-only with fair value anchored near 0.785 rather than chasing a richer price.
audit.notes: This refresh is a pure valuation update: same directional Yes, weaker execution case.
